decisions.what: Thread projectId from render scope into itemHtml/advChangeHtml/changeStatusHtml/groupHtml/sourceDetailsHtml rather than embedding project id on every lane item
decisions.why: Lane item types (ChangeStatusLaneItem/AdvChangeLaneItem) carry changeId but no project id; render() already iterates state.projects so project.id is available at render time. Avoids touching backend item shapes (attention.ts) — keeps change UI-only.

decisions.what: Build detail links via changeDetailHref using encodeURIComponent on both project id and change id, prefixed '/change/'
decisions.why: Same-origin local route the server already serves; encoding makes the path safe; escapeHtml on the resulting href prevents attribute injection. No external links, no target=_blank for detail.

decisions.what: Render deeper context as escaped JSON inside <pre> within a <details> disclosure, only when detail.deeper != null
decisions.why: deeper is unknown/arbitrary shape from the read model; escaping as text (never raw HTML) prevents injection while staying read-only and collapsed by default.

decisions.what: sourceHealthHtml reads affected/project/repo and remediation/message defensively with fallbacks; labels block 'Source health' and surfaces source+code+last_success_at
decisions.why: DashboardDegradedSource only guarantees source/code/message/last_success_at; optional affected/remediation fields render when present without crashing. Framed as source-health (not ADV change failure) per task requirement.

subagent_reports.related_scan: Scanned all itemHtml dispatch callers (renderLane, sourceDetailsHtml, groupHtml) and updated every call site to the new projectId-threaded signature consistently; verified no other dispatcher of adv_change/adv_change_status/group exists in ui.ts. Updated two pre-existing ui.test.ts assertions that pinned the old single-arg dispatch lines.

context_update_for_adv.what_ads_needs_to_know: Dashboard UI now: (1) ADV cards (adv_change + adv_change_status) link to same-origin /change/{projectId}/{changeId} via escaped, encoded local href; (2) same HTML shell renders detail mode — on a /change/ path it fetches /api/change/{projectId}/{changeId} (GET) instead of /api/state and renders compact read-only fields (title/id/project/next gate/progress/status/lastActivity/branches/paths/copyable adv_change_show command), with deeper context in a <details> disclosure when present; (3) detail degradedSources render as Source-health cards (source/code/affected/remediation/last_success_at), explicitly not framed as ADV change failure. Read-only preserved: no rerun/merge/approve/deploy/archive/cancel controls, no forms/POST, escaping + safeUrl kept, no raw ADV state-file links. UI verified deterministically via bun tests; no browser/Playwright run (string-template render, no live server needed for these assertions).
context_update_for_adv.suggested_next_action: Proceed to acceptance/review for hardenDashboardReads; optionally add a live server smoke (open /change/<pid>/<cid>) if browser evidence is desired, but unit coverage already verifies fetch target, render shape, and safety invariants.

design_dimensions.notes: Detail link uses <a> with visible focus-visible underline (keyboard reachable); detail page uses semantic <h2>, <dl>/<dt>/<dd> grid, <ul> for branch/path lists, and <details>/<summary> disclosures for deeper context and source-health. Reused existing design tokens (--accent/--warning/--surface/--border) and card/disclosure idioms so detail view matches the dashboard. Detail card constrained to 60rem max-width with overflow-x scroll on code/pre blocks for narrow viewports. Degraded sources visually distinct (warning-tinted source-health card) and clearly labeled as source health, not change failure.

sources.label: bin/lib/live-status.ts firstSearchAttribute
sources.summary: searchAttributeString collapses KeywordList to element [0]. Promoting multi-branch AdvWorktreeBranches into the summary needs an array-aware reader, not searchAttributeString.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Proposal thesis is validated by authoritative Temporal docs: handle.query needs a live Worker; ListWorkflow over Visibility search attributes is server-side and worker-free. The remaining worker-coupled path is readDashboardAdvProject's loadOpsChanges ?? loadLiveStatus (live-status.ts:64-66), which fans out one getState query per change. The boring, by-the-book fix is to source what the dashboard needs from Visibility search attributes that ADV already upserts. Crucially, AdvWorktreeBranches/AdvWorktreePaths are ALREADY promoted (search-attributes.ts:214-215) but buildSummaryFromSearchAttributes ignores them — so branch correlation can move to the worker-free path with no new promoted attributes and no schema change. The genuine trade-off needing a decision: head_shas and ops_followup/ops_followup_links have NO Visibility attribute today, so fully deleting loadLiveStatus from routine refresh drops head-SHA correlation and ops lane evidence unless (a) new search attributes are promoted, or (b) those are demoted to a bounded, explicit user-triggered detail query — which the proposal's Discovery Agenda explicitly lists as an acceptable target. For click-through, the proposal already permits a bounded explicit user-triggered query for details; a read-only GET /api/change/:id route reusing one getState query is the safe, minimal shape, staying within loopback + read-only + no-mutation constraints.

validation.notes: No contract/security/release blocker. One decision is needed from orchestrator/user (head_shas + ops lane evidence fate when removing routine per-change fan-out) — captured as a surface candidate, not a blocker. Note firstSearchAttribute/searchAttributeString collapse KeywordList to [0]; promoting branches needs an array-aware reader to avoid silently dropping multi-worktree branches.

subagent_reports.recommendation: AUTO-ADOPT: populate worktreeBranches (and optionally worktreePaths) in buildSummaryFromSearchAttributes from the already-upserted AdvWorktreeBranches/AdvWorktreePaths KeywordList via a new array-aware search-attribute reader (do not reuse searchAttributeString, which keeps only element 0). This removes the only routine reliance on loadLiveStatus for branch correlation, is schema-neutral, and aligns adv.test.ts's existing expectation with production. SURFACE to user/orchestrator: decide the fate of head_shas + ops_followup/ops_followup_links on routine refresh — either (1) promote a head-SHA/ops search attribute so they stay worker-free, or (2) demote them to a bounded explicit user-triggered detail query (GET /api/change/:id, read-only, loopback, one getState). For click-through, implement that same bounded read-only detail route rather than enriching every refresh. REJECT scope creep into removing loadLiveStatus entirely without first resolving head_shas/ops fate.

sources.label: Temporal TS SDK docs — typed search attributes (Context7 /temporalio/sdk-typescript)
sources.summary: KeywordList typed value is a native string[] (value: ['typed','keywords']). Confirms AdvWorktreeBranches/AdvWorktreePaths arrive as arrays; the design needs an array-aware reader, not a bespoke binary decoder.

sources.label: bin/lib/live-status.ts firstSearchAttribute/searchAttributeString
sources.summary: firstSearchAttribute ALREADY array-detects and collapses to element [0]; searchAttributeString wraps it. A 5-line array-returning sibling (return the whole filtered string[]) is all that's needed — not a new 'KeywordList decoder' module.

sources.label: bin/lib/dashboard/adv.ts readDashboardAdvProject ops-enrichment seam
sources.summary: loadOpsChanges ?? loadLiveStatus is ALREADY wrapped in try/catch that degrades to ADV_OPS_ENRICHMENT_UNAVAILABLE. mergeAdvChange already reads summary.worktreeBranches (line 96, 102-105). The injection seam + degradation the design wants mostly EXISTS; the change is removing the routine default + populating summary branches upstream.

sources.label: plugin/src/temporal/observability.ts buildSearchAttributes
sources.summary: AdvWorktreeBranches/AdvWorktreePaths are upserted (sorted string[]). Singleton Keyword attrs are also array-wrapped ([input.changeStatus]) — which is why legacy Record reads collapse to [0]. Multi-element KeywordList is the only case needing array-aware reads.

sources.label: bin/lib/live-status.ts listLiveChangeStates single-change query
sources.summary: The per-change getHandle(id).query(getState) lives in a loop. A detail route can reuse exactly ONE iteration of this for a single change — the canonical bounded worker-coupled read — instead of authoring a new query path.

sources.label: bin/lib/dashboard/config.ts sanitizeDashboardState + ui.ts inline escapeHtml/safeUrl
sources.summary: sanitizeDashboardState recursively strips token-like material (config.test.ts:101). escapeHtml/safeUrl exist ONLY as inline functions inside the client HTML string — not importable server helpers. Detail API must route through sanitizeDashboardState; detail HTML must reuse the same inline escape pattern.

sources.label: bin/lib/dashboard/server.ts createDashboardHandler
sources.summary: Handler rejects non-GET (405) before routing, serves only / and /api/state, loopback enforced. A GET /change/... or /api/change/... clause slots in here with zero new safety scaffolding.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: The draft design is directionally correct and contract-aligned, but overstates the amount of NEW surface required — three of its planks are mostly already-present seams. (1) 'Add a KeywordList decoder in live-status.ts': over-scoped. firstSearchAttribute (live-status.ts:216-224) already array-detects; the minimal, boring fix is a sibling searchAttributeStringList(attrs,key) returning the full filtered string[], then setting summary.worktreeBranches/worktreePaths in buildSummaryFromSearchAttributes (live-status.ts:288-301). Temporal docs confirm KeywordList values are native string[], so no payload/binary decoding is involved. (2) 'Remove routine loadLiveStatus ops enrichment': the try/catch degradation (adv.ts:64-71) and the loadOpsChanges injection seam (adv.ts:36,65) ALREADY exist, and mergeAdvChange already merges summary.worktreeBranches (adv.ts:96). The real change is narrow: stop defaulting loadOpsChanges to loadLiveStatus on routine refresh (make ops enrichment opt-in/absent) and rely on the now-populated summary branches — reuse the existing sentinel seam for the 'no fan-out' test rather than inventing one. (3) Detail route: the bounded one-change read should be a single reuse of listLiveChangeStates' inner getHandle(id).query(getState) (live-status.ts:99-108), not a new query path; compact level draws from the /api/state snapshot already in hand. Safety is free: GET-only + loopback gate already sit in createDashboardHandler; sanitizeDashboardState already redacts secrets; escapeHtml/safeUrl already exist but ONLY inline in ui.ts — the detail page must reuse that same inline pattern, and the detail JSON must pass through sanitizeDashboardState. Net: the design's user value is achievable with markedly less new code than the draft implies; the leverage is in reusing existing seams (injection point, degradation path, single-change query, sanitizer, GET/loopback gate) rather than building parallel mechanisms.

validation.notes: No contract/security/release blocker. Two design-shaping recommendations for the orchestrator before planning: (a) scope the KeywordList work as a small array-aware reader, not a decoder module — avoids inventing parallel decoding; (b) head_shas + ops_followup/ops_followup_links have NO Visibility attribute, so removing routine loadLiveStatus drops head-SHA + ops-lane correlation from routine cards unless demoted to the detail route's bounded one-change read (recommended) or promoted as new search attrs (heavier, hits the 3-KeywordList dev-server cap noted at search-attributes.ts:6-12). The agreement already permits user-triggered bounded detail reads, so demotion is the boring path.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Before validation/planning, tighten the design along existing seams: (1) Replace 'KeywordList decoder' with a 5-line array-aware searchAttributeStringList reader beside firstSearchAttribute; populate summary.worktreeBranches/worktreePaths in buildSummaryFromSearchAttributes. (2) Frame the loadLiveStatus removal as 'drop the routine default for loadOpsChanges + rely on summary branches', reusing the existing try/catch degradation and injection sentinel for the no-fan-out test. (3) Implement the detail route by reusing ONE iteration of listLiveChangeStates' single-change getState query for expandable depth, compact level from the /api/state snapshot; gate it through the existing GET-only + loopback handler and pass its JSON through sanitizeDashboardState, reusing ui.ts inline escapeHtml/safeUrl for HTML. (4) Demote head_shas + ops_followup correlation to the bounded detail read rather than promoting new KeywordList search attrs (dev-server cap is 3, already full).
follow_ups: All packet anchors (TASK_SCOPE/IN_SCOPE/OUT_OF_SCOPE/DONE_WHEN/STOP_WHEN/VERIFICATION) present; no missing-anchor warning.

sources.label: Spec rq-statusCliWorkerFree01 (advance-meta)
sources.summary: Established ADV law: status table must build active-change rows from Temporal Visibility search attributes worker-free, NOT per-change getState query (which needs live worker). Design directly mirrors this for /api/state. Strong precedent.

sources.label: bin/lib/live-status.ts:87-112 listLiveChangeStates
sources.summary: Confirms current anti-pattern: loops change IDs and issues per-change workflow.query(getState). This is the fan-out the design removes from routine /api/state path.

sources.label: bin/lib/live-status.ts:216-235 firstSearchAttribute/searchAttributeString
sources.summary: Existing helper already does Array.isArray(value) handling, proving KeywordList attrs arrive as JS arrays. searchAttributeStringList extension is a trivial, low-risk addition.

sources.label: bin/lib/dashboard/adv.ts:59-104 readDashboardAdvProject
sources.summary: Confirms default path: loadBase=loadLiveSummaries then loadOps=loadLiveStatus fan-out. Merge already reads summary.worktreeBranches (line 96-104), so projecting AdvWorktreeBranches into ChangeSummary is wiring an existing consumer.

sources.label: Temporal TS SDK typed search attributes
sources.summary: KEYWORD_LIST value type is string[] (e.g. ['typed','keywords']). Validates design claim that KeywordList decodes to array values in list results; no custom decoder module needed.

sources.label: AdvWorktreeBranches/AdvWorktreePaths registration
sources.summary: Both confirmed as registered KeywordList search attributes (refactorChangeWorkflowsSignal T15), upserted by change workflows. No new search attrs required for branches/paths.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design is a by-the-book read-model split that extends an already-ratified ADV pattern (rq-statusCliWorkerFree01) from the CLI status table to the dashboard /api/state path. Every load-bearing technical claim verified against actual code: the getState fan-out exists and is the right target to remove; KeywordList attrs already arrive as arrays and the existing helper already array-handles; worktree branch/path search attributes already exist and are registered; the dashboard merge already consumes summary.worktreeBranches. The detail-route split (user-triggered, single-change bounded read, locally degraded, independent from /api/state) is the canonical CQRS-style summary-vs-detail separation and satisfies every avoidance. Deferring new ops/head-SHA search attributes until need is proven is correct YAGNI-aligned restraint that avoids widening a small Temporal search-attribute surface. No simpler equivalent exists that still satisfies the worker-free + preserve-context + read-only constraints together. No spec contradiction found.

validation.notes: VALIDATED. Two non-blocking cautions worth confirming in prep, neither a design defect: (1) AC3 'degraded' wording vs design decision-3 'omit' wording for ops/head-SHA — design omits these from routine cards rather than rendering an explicit degraded marker; reconcile whether AC3's 'omitted OR degraded' is satisfied by silent omission or whether a lightweight 'not loaded' marker is expected, to avoid an acceptance-matrix ambiguity. (2) Recorded clarify finding CLARIFY_ASSUMPTION_HEAVY on auth model is correctly neutralized by the agreement constraint 'no GitHub authentication model change' + local-only/loopback GET-only posture; no new auth surface is introduced, so the click-through detail route inherits the existing same-origin loopback trust boundary — confirm the detail route reuses the same loopback/GET-only guard rather than introducing a new bind.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Proceed to planning. The design is correct, simple, and spec-aligned: it generalizes the existing worker-free Visibility law (rq-statusCliWorkerFree01) to the dashboard and removes a verified per-change getState fan-out while preserving card context via already-registered worktree KeywordList attributes that the merge layer already expects. No major alternative was overlooked — promoting ops/head-SHA to new search attributes was correctly rejected as premature, and a full read-model persistence rewrite would be overengineering for this scope. During prep, (a) add a regression test with a sentinel loadOps that fails if invoked on the default /api/state path (design already calls for this), (b) reconcile AC3 'omitted vs degraded' wording for ops/head-SHA so the acceptance matrix is unambiguous, and (c) assert the new detail route reuses the existing loopback + GET-only + safeUrl/escapeHtml guards rather than adding a new server binding. Consider an explicit spec-law update in advance-meta extending the worker-free routine-refresh law to the dashboard surface, so the new /api/state contract is durably enforced, not only test-enforced.
follow_ups: Prep: confirm AC3 acceptance wording — silent omission vs explicit 'not loaded' degraded marker for ops/head-SHA on routine cards.
Prep: assert detail route /change + /api/change reuse existing loopback + GET-only 405 + safeUrl/escapeHtml guards (no new bind, no new auth surface).
Prep: add sentinel loadOps regression test ensuring default /api/state never triggers getState fan-out.
Optional: extend advance-meta spec with a dashboard worker-free routine-refresh law so the /api/state contract is spec-enforced, not only test-enforced.

documents.proposal:
# Harden dashboard reads

## Why

The local ADV dashboard has become useful as a live read-only view, but two related gaps remain:

1. Temporal safety: base ADV dashboard state uses Temporal Visibility, but ops enrichment still depends on per-change workflow state queries through the `loadLiveStatus` path. That can make dashboard refreshes worker-dependent and scale with the number of changes.
2. Navigation: ADV cards show useful status but do not provide a safe way to inspect the underlying change context from the dashboard.

The intended outcome is a dashboard that remains local/read-only while following Temporal read-model best practices and giving users a safe inspection path for ADV changes.

## What Changes

- Audit and adjust the dashboard ADV data path so refreshes do not rely on per-change workflow query fan-out for routine state.
- Preserve degraded behavior when optional enrichment cannot be read.
- Add a safe read-only navigation path from ADV cards to change details or local inspection context.
- Keep GitHub degraded-source cards distinct from ADV change attention cards.

## User Outcomes

- Users can trust the dashboard not to add avoidable load or worker coupling to Temporal.
- Users can click an ADV card to inspect more context for that change without leaving the local read-only dashboard model.
- Users can distinguish real ADV/change attention from temporary source degradation such as GitHub HTTP 401.
- Dashboard remains fast, local, and non-mutating as active change counts grow.

## Affected Code

- `bin/lib/live-status.ts`
- `bin/lib/dashboard/adv.ts`
- `bin/lib/dashboard/server.ts`
- `bin/lib/dashboard/ui.ts`
- `bin/lib/dashboard/*test.ts`
- Potentially `bin/adv` if dashboard routes or config surface need wiring

## Related Repositories

- Current repository only: `/home/jon/dev/advance`
- PokeEdge/PokeEdge-Web remain observed dashboard targets, not implementation targets for this change.

## Constraints

- Local-only dashboard.
- Read-only dashboard; no mutation controls.
- No tokens or secrets displayed.
- No direct raw ADV state-file reads or links.
- No authentication model change; existing GitHub token/CLI auth behavior remains out of scope except for safe degraded display.
- Read failures must degrade the affected source/detail surface instead of crashing the whole dashboard; no rollback flow is needed for read-only presentation changes.
- Avoid Temporal anti-patterns: dashboard refresh should not require per-change workflow query fan-out, unbounded polling, or a live worker for ordinary summary state.
- Preserve bounded refresh/cache/coalescing behavior.

## Impact

- Improves reliability and scalability of the local dashboard.
- Reduces chance that dashboard UI reads are confused with workflow orchestration authority.
- Adds discoverability for ADV change context while preserving ADV state access boundaries.

## Context

Observed facts from the current dashboard audit:

- Base status summaries are produced from Temporal Visibility search attributes in `summariesFromVisibility` / `loadLiveSummaries`.
- Dashboard state provider caches/coalesces `/api/state` refreshes and the browser refreshes at bounded intervals.
- `readDashboardAdvProject` still attempts ops enrichment with `loadOpsChanges ?? loadLiveStatus`, which is the remaining per-change workflow query path.
- The screenshot `Needs attention` item was a `degraded_source` from GitHub HTTP 401, not an ADV change; later refresh cleared it.
- `adv_status` timed out during preflight, while `adv_change_list` and Temporal diagnostics succeeded. Diagnostic reported server reachable, local worker not running, peer workers serviceable.

## Discovery Findings

### Discovery Checklist

| Step | Result | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| Skill Discovery | PASS | Considered `adv-arch-detection`, default lgrep guidance, and Temporal docs. Loaded `adv-arch-detection`; no new skill needed. |
| Prior Research Extension | PASS | Cited `docs/repo-improve-prep.md`; added new dashboard-specific findings around indirect worker-query enrichment and unused worktree Visibility attrs. |
| Conflict & Related-Work Scan | PASS with degraded related-list | `adv_change_validate` passed with expected `NO_TASKS`/`NO_DELTAS` warnings. `adv_change_list includeArchived` timed out twice; known related archived dashboard changes were inspected directly. Agenda includes overlapping `Optimize adv-status for large WIP sets`. |
| Edge Case Investigation | PASS | Edge cases captured for Temporal read path, click-through, and degraded source UX. |
| Design Question Depth | PASS | Questions annotated and resolved or carried to design. |
| Draft Spec Delta Shapes | PASS | Draft spec delta proposed for worker-free dashboard routine refresh. |
| Related Pattern Scan | PASS | Found similar worker-free status CLI law and perf research pack; no other dashboard routine query fan-out found beyond `readDashboardAdvProject` enrichment. |
| LBP Check | PASS | Visibility/read-model routine refresh + bounded on-demand detail read is long-term best practice. |
| Completeness Verification | PASS | Problem includes Temporal safety, card inspection, and degraded-source clarity; secondary surfaces classified. |

### Skills Considered

- `adv-arch-detection`: matched architecture inconsistency / P33 boundary audit. Loaded and applied conceptually: deterministic evidence first, heuristic findings only with file refs.
- `lgrep`: already always-on; used for exact code surface search rather than broad glob-first exploration.
- `adv-worktree`: not loaded; worktree behavior is detail metadata only, not core workflow.
- No skill gap created; Temporal/dashboard read-model guidance is covered by code evidence + Temporal docs + existing project specs.

### Extends

- `docs/repo-improve-prep.md`: prior research already identifies Visibility-first status paths as sound and full hydration/query-heavy status paths as anti-patterns. New finding: local dashboard base summaries follow this direction, but `readDashboardAdvProject` indirectly reintroduces per-change `getState` fan-out through `loadLiveStatus` for optional ops/worktree enrichment.
- Archived `reframeDashboardStatus`: established current dashboard lanes are ADV-change centered and unmatched source remains secondary.
- Archived `improveDashboardCards`: established cards now show gate progress and sort by completed gates.

### Conflict Scan

- `adv_change_validate`: passed; warnings `NO_TASKS` and `NO_DELTAS` expected pre-discovery.
- `adv_change_list includeArchived`: timed out twice, likely same hot-read scale issue implicated by the change. Known related dashboard changes inspected directly: `reframeDashboardStatus`, `improveDashboardCards`.
- Agenda: relevant overlap `ag-Pu9AaBst` / “Optimize adv-status for large WIP sets”; related to read-path performance but scoped to `adv_status`, not dashboard.
- Scout added agenda notes for branch/path Visibility projection and ops/head-sha fate.

### Current State

- `bin/lib/live-status.ts` has two paths:
  - `loadLiveSummaries` → `summariesFromVisibility` → server-side Visibility attrs, worker-free.
  - `loadLiveStatus` → `listLiveChangeStates` → per-workflow `getState` query fan-out.
- `bin/lib/dashboard/adv.ts` calls `loadBaseSummaries` first, then optional `loadOpsChanges ?? loadLiveStatus`; failure degrades as `ADV_OPS_ENRICHMENT_UNAVAILABLE` while base cards remain visible.
- `plugin/src/temporal/search-attributes.ts` already upserts `AdvWorktreeBranches` and `AdvWorktreePaths` from non-deleted worktrees.
- `buildSummaryFromSearchAttributes` currently ignores `AdvWorktreeBranches`/`AdvWorktreePaths`; `searchAttributeString` only reads the first value and is not suitable for KeywordList multi-values.
- `bin/lib/dashboard/adv.test.ts` already has a fixture expecting `worktreeBranches` on `ChangeSummary`, but production does not populate it from Visibility.
- Dashboard server currently serves only `/` and `/api/state`, rejects non-GET, binds loopback by default, and tests cache/coalesce `/api/state`.
- UI cards are rendered from client-side HTML strings with safe URL allowlisting and escaping.

### Edge Cases

Temporal/read path:
- No local worker or worker query handler unavailable: routine `/api/state` must still render from Visibility or degrade clearly.
- Many active changes: refresh must not issue one workflow query per change.
- `RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED` or query buffer pressure: SDK retries can amplify latency if dashboard fans out queries.
- Multi-worktree changes: KeywordList attrs need array-aware decoding, not first-value collapse.
- Missing or stale Visibility attrs: dashboard should degrade or omit optional detail, not infer false source correlation.

Click-through/detail:
- Unknown change id or project id: detail route returns bounded not-found/degraded state, not crash.
- Detail read timeout: detail page shows compact fallback and copyable inspection command.
- Raw state paths: must not link directly to external ADV state files.
- Detail route must remain GET-only and not add controls.

Degraded source UX:
- GitHub HTTP 401: show source, affected repo, auth context/remediation, and last successful refresh if available.
- Per-project failure: only affected project shows degradation.
- Degraded source must not be mistaken for an ADV change requiring gate/action work.

### Open Design Questions

1. Final detail-read source
   - Trust model: agent-owned technical choice.
   - Blast radius: too much detail can reintroduce worker coupling; too little detail makes click-through unhelpful.
   - Alternatives: Visibility-only detail, bounded on-demand `getState`, or no detail. Recommendation: routine refresh stays Visibility-only; detail route may use a bounded one-change read only after explicit user click and degrades safely.

2. Ops/head-sha fate
   - Trust model: agent-owned unless user requires ops fields in dashboard cards.
   - Blast radius: removing per-refresh ops query can drop optional ops/head-sha enrichment; preserving it recreates fan-out.
   - Alternatives: promote ops/head-sha to Visibility/read model, fetch only on detail route, or omit from dashboard. Recommendation: no per-refresh query; use existing branch/path Visibility now, and make deeper ops/head-sha detail best-effort on click.

3. Degraded-source placement/context
   - Trust model: user-facing, user answered.
   - Blast radius: cryptic degraded card looks like a change failure; hidden source failures make dashboard misleading.
   - Alternatives: Needs attention card, project header only, or source-health/detail card. Resolution: show more specific/detailed context and distinguish it from ADV change attention.

4. Card click target/depth
   - Trust model: user-facing, user answered.
   - Blast radius: wrong target interrupts local dashboard workflow or violates state boundaries.
   - Alternatives: local detail page, command panel, source links first. Resolution: local detail page with two levels: compact default plus expandable detail.

### Draft Spec Deltas

Proposed target: `advance-meta` or a new dashboard-local spec if design finds dashboard behavior warrants its own capability.

- `rq-dashboardWorkerFree01` — Dashboard routine refresh uses worker-free read model.
  - Given the local dashboard requests `/api/state`, when Temporal Visibility is reachable, then ADV change summary cards are built without per-change workflow `getState` queries.
  - Given optional enrichment is unavailable from Visibility/read model, when `/api/state` renders, then optional enrichment is omitted or degraded without hiding base ADV cards.
  - Given the dashboard provides click-through change details, when a detail route needs richer state, then any worker-dependent read is bounded, one-change scoped, user-triggered, and degrades visibly.

### Related Pattern Scan

- `rq-statusCliWorkerFree01` already requires `adv status` to build active rows from Visibility search attributes instead of per-change workflow queries.
- `plugin/src/cli-bridge-contract.test.ts` checks CLI direct use of `loadLiveStatus`, but dashboard imports `loadLiveStatus` indirectly through `readDashboardAdvProject` and is not covered by the same no-fanout law.
- `loadLiveStatus(` appears only in `bin/lib/live-status.ts`; the dashboard path uses it through the default `loadOpsChanges` dependency, so the issue is localized.
- `AdvWorktreeBranches`/`AdvWorktreePaths` are already registered/upserted and used by worktree tools, so projecting them into `ChangeSummary` is a same-pattern correction.

### LBP Check

- Best practice: routine dashboard refresh should use server-side Visibility/read-model data, not live workflow queries per row.
- Temporal docs evidence: Workflow queries are read-only but execute through workflow query RPC and can be rejected/unavailable; SDK retries `RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED`/`UNAVAILABLE`, so fan-out can amplify latency under pressure. Visibility search attributes are server-side list/query metadata and are the correct basis for summary dashboards.
- Chosen direction: Visibility/read-model for `/api/state`; one-change bounded best-effort detail read only after user click if design needs richer data.
- External alternatives: skipped; this is internal Temporal/dashboard architecture with no viable external service/library decision.

### Discovery Opportunity Scout

- Trigger: run — architecture/read-model change with Temporal correctness risk.
- Candidates considered: 5.
- Auto-adopted: populate `worktreeBranches`/`worktreePaths` in `buildSummaryFromSearchAttributes` from existing `AdvWorktreeBranches`/`AdvWorktreePaths` using array-aware decoding.
- User-surfaced/resolved: click target and detail depth; user selected local detail page and two-level detail.
- Design-carry: ops/head-sha enrichment should not remain in routine refresh; design decides whether to promote fields to Visibility or make them best-effort detail-only.

### Completeness Verification

Problem-completeness:
- The full problem is broader than the single screenshot card: it includes worker-coupled routine dashboard reads, missing safe change inspection, and unclear degraded-source context.
- Confidence: high; evidence spans proposal, live dashboard behavior, Temporal read code, server routes, and prior dashboard changes.

Solution-scope:
- The full intended solution is not “remove one function call.” It needs a routine-refresh read-model guarantee, safe detail route, and source-health UX.
- Sole-entry claim: routine dashboard state is served through `createDashboardStateProvider` / `buildDashboardState` for `/api/state`; verified from `bin/lib/dashboard/server.ts` and tests. Static HTML route `/` has no state read.
- Secondary surfaces:
  - `adv status` CLI: out of scope; already governed by `rq-statusCliWorkerFree01`.
  - GitHub reader: in scope only for degraded-source display clarity, not auth model changes.
  - Dashboard detail route: in scope, but user-triggered and not routine refresh.

### AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS

Coverage: B:C F:C S:C M:C

No blocking ambiguity findings.

Advisory note: exact detail-field set is design-owned; user has approved local detail page + two-level detail outcome, so this is not a discovery blocker.

## Scope

### In Scope

- Dashboard ADV read-path audit and refinement.
- Removing, replacing, or gating per-change workflow query fan-out from routine dashboard refresh.
- Read-only ADV card click-through/details for local inspection.
- Dashboard tests covering Temporal-read behavior, degraded behavior, and click-through safety.

### Out of Scope

- Mutation actions from the dashboard.
- Hosted dashboard service.
- Changes to PokeEdge or PokeEdge-Web application code.
- Bulk redesign of all ADV persistence or workflow internals.
- Raw external ADV state-file browsing from dashboard links.
- GitHub authentication model changes.

### Must Not

- Must not add signals, updates, task mutations, archive controls, rerun controls, merge controls, or deploy controls.
- Must not rely on per-change workflow query fan-out for ordinary refresh summary state.
- Must not expose secrets, tokens, raw auth errors with sensitive content, or raw state-file paths as primary navigation.
- Must not make the dashboard depend on a live local worker when Temporal Visibility can provide the needed summary state.
documents.problemStatement: The local ADV dashboard needs to remain a safe read-only live view while avoiding Temporal anti-patterns and making ADV cards inspectable. Today the base state uses Visibility, but optional ops enrichment still has a per-change workflow query path, and cards do not provide a safe click-through path to change context.

# Agreement

## Objectives

1. Make routine dashboard ADV summary refresh worker-free and Visibility/read-model based.
2. Preserve useful ADV card context while removing per-change workflow query fan-out from `/api/state`.
3. Add read-only ADV card click-through to a local detail view.
4. Improve degraded-source context so source failures are specific and not confused with ADV change failures.
5. Preserve local-only, read-only, no-secret dashboard behavior.

## Success Criteria

1. Routine dashboard refresh is worker-free for ADV summary state.
2. ADV cards open a local read-only detail view.
3. Detail view has two levels: compact default + expandable deeper context.
4. Source degradation is specific and actionable, not a cryptic ADV-change-looking card.
5. Existing dashboard behavior remains local-only, read-only, bounded, cached/coalesced, and secret-safe.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Given `/api/state` builds ADV summary cards, when Temporal Visibility is reachable, then routine refresh does not call per-change workflow `getState` queries.
2. Given a change has `AdvWorktreeBranches` or `AdvWorktreePaths` Visibility attributes, when dashboard summaries are built, then branch/path data is decoded from Visibility without worker queries.
3. Given optional ops/head-SHA enrichment is unavailable from Visibility/read-model state, when `/api/state` renders, then base ADV cards remain visible and optional enrichment is omitted or degraded without per-change query fan-out.
4. Given a user clicks an ADV card, when the local detail route opens, then it shows compact change context by default and expandable deeper context without mutation controls.
5. Given a detail route needs richer state than Visibility provides, when that read fails or times out, then the detail page degrades with a clear message and copyable inspection command instead of crashing or blocking `/api/state`.
6. Given GitHub or ADV source reads fail, when the dashboard renders, then source-health degradation shows source, affected repo/project, code, specific context/remediation, and last-success info when known, without being presented as an ADV change failure.
7. Given dashboard HTML renders links, commands, source data, or errors, when values contain unsafe URLs, markup, or token-like content, then unsafe URLs are suppressed, HTML is escaped, and secrets/tokens are not displayed.
8. Given non-GET requests hit dashboard state/detail routes, when the server handles them, then they are rejected and no ADV/GitHub/Temporal mutation path exists.
9. Given dashboard tests run, when targeted dashboard suites complete, then worker-free routine refresh, Visibility branch/path projection, detail route degradation, source-health context, and read-only/no-secret constraints are covered.

## Constraints

1. Local-only dashboard.
2. Read-only dashboard; no mutation controls.
3. No tokens or secrets displayed.
4. No direct raw ADV state-file reads or links.
5. No GitHub authentication model change.
6. Read failures degrade affected source/detail surface instead of crashing the whole dashboard.
7. Routine refresh must not rely on per-change workflow query fan-out.
8. Routine summary state must not require a live local worker when Temporal Visibility can provide needed state.
9. Preserve bounded refresh/cache/coalescing behavior.

## Avoidances

1. Do not add signals, updates, task mutations, archive controls, rerun controls, merge controls, or deploy controls.
2. Do not use per-change workflow query fan-out for ordinary refresh summary state.
3. Do not expose secrets, tokens, raw auth errors with sensitive content, or raw state-file paths as primary navigation.
4. Do not make `/api/state` depend on a live local worker for base ADV cards.
5. Do not turn source-health degradation into an ADV change failure card.

## Preview Applicability

visual_surface: true

Rationale: The change affects the browser-visible local dashboard UI, including card links/detail pages and source-health display.

## Decisions

### User Decisions

- Question: What should clicking an ADV card open?
  - User choice: Local detail page.
  - Why it matters: defines navigation outcome and avoids raw state-file links.
- Question: How much detail should the local ADV change page show by default?
  - User choice: Two levels.
  - Why it matters: compact default avoids noise while expandable detail preserves inspection depth.
- Question: When source reads fail, where/how should degradation show?
  - User choice: More specific and detailed context.
  - Why it matters: cryptic `GITHUB_HTTP_401` cards are not actionable and can be mistaken for ADV change failures.

### Agent Decisions (LBP)

- Routine `/api/state` should use Temporal Visibility/read-model data only for ADV summaries.
- `AdvWorktreeBranches` and `AdvWorktreePaths` should be decoded from existing Visibility KeywordList attrs with array-aware parsing.
- Optional ops/head-SHA enrichment should not remain a per-refresh worker-query dependency; design may promote fields to read model or make deeper details best-effort/on-demand.
- One-change detail reads, if needed, must be user-triggered, bounded, read-only, and degraded independently from `/api/state`.
- Existing dashboard safe URL, HTML escaping, and GET-only server patterns remain the safety baseline.

## Deferred Questions

None.

## Sign-Off

Criteria approved by user reply `approve` during discovery checkpoint.
documents.design:
# Design

## Architecture Overview

The dashboard read model splits into two paths:

1. **Routine summary path** — `/api/state`
   - Uses Temporal Visibility/search-attribute summaries only for ADV cards.
   - Does not call per-change workflow `getState` queries.
   - Provides enough data for cards, lane placement, branches, worktree paths, source correlation, and safe click links.

2. **User-triggered detail path** — `/change/{projectId}/{changeId}` + `/api/change/{projectId}/{changeId}`
   - Opens from an ADV card click.
   - Compact default view comes from the same read-model shape.
   - Expandable deeper context may use one bounded read of that single change when available.
   - Failure degrades only the detail panel/page and never blocks `/api/state`.

This preserves a worker-free dashboard refresh while still allowing richer inspection after explicit user action.

## Key Decisions

### 1. Remove routine ops enrichment fan-out from `/api/state`

`readDashboardAdvProject` currently calls `loadBaseSummaries` and then attempts `loadOpsChanges ?? loadLiveStatus`. `loadLiveStatus` lists change workflows then queries each workflow's `getState`. That is safe as a read, but it is wrong for a dashboard refresh because it scales with change count and requires a worker.

Decision: routine dashboard summaries use `loadLiveSummaries`/Visibility only. Optional ops/head-SHA enrichment is not fetched during `/api/state` refresh.

Rationale:
- Matches `rq-statusCliWorkerFree01` direction already established for `adv status`.
- Avoids SDK retry amplification under `RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED`/`UNAVAILABLE`.
- Keeps `/api/state` usable when no local worker is polling.

### 2. Project existing worktree Visibility attrs into summaries

`AdvWorktreeBranches` and `AdvWorktreePaths` already exist and are upserted by change workflows. Production summaries currently ignore them.

Decision: add a small array-aware search-attribute helper next to `searchAttributeString`, then project:

- `worktreeBranches?: string[]`
- `worktreePaths?: string[]`

into `ChangeSummary` from `AdvWorktreeBranches` / `AdvWorktreePaths`.

Rationale:
- No new Temporal search attributes required.
- No decoder module needed; Temporal SDK exposes KeywordList as `string[]`/array values in list results.
- Existing dashboard merge logic already looks for `summary.worktreeBranches`.

### 3. Demote ops/head-SHA detail to explicit detail read

`head_shas`, `ops_followup`, and `ops_followup_links` do not currently have Visibility attrs. Adding more KeywordList attrs now risks overusing a small search-attribute surface and is not required for routine card correctness.

Decision: routine cards omit or mark these as unavailable. If detail needs them, fetch them only through a bounded one-change read on the detail route.

Rationale:
- Keeps routine refresh worker-free.
- Preserves deeper inspection when worker/query path is available.
- Avoids widening Temporal search-attribute schema before proving sustained need.

### 4. Implement detail as local read-only page + JSON API

ADV cards link to `/change/{projectId}/{changeId}`. The route serves the same dashboard HTML shell or a detail-focused HTML shell. The client fetches `/api/change/{projectId}/{changeId}`.

Detail JSON contains:
- compact base fields: id, title, lifecycle/status, gate, progress, last activity
- branch/worktree paths from Visibility
- latest linked source summaries already available from the dashboard model when possible
- optional deeper fields when bounded one-change read succeeds
- degraded detail status when richer read fails/times out
- copyable command: `adv_change_show(changeId: "{id}")`

No direct raw ADV state path is linked.

### 5. Source-health degradation is a first-class source-health card

Current degraded cards can appear in Needs attention and look like ADV failures. The source-health display needs more context.

Decision: render degraded source items with explicit context:
- source: github/adv
- affected repo or project label/path
- code
- user-readable explanation
- remediation
- last successful refresh if available

They remain separate from `adv_change_status` cards and cannot be mistaken for an ADV gate/action failure.

## ADR Drafts

None. Decisions are important but not hard-to-reverse enough to require an ADR; they are localized to dashboard read architecture and can be revised by later dashboard changes.

## Implementation Strategy

1. **Visibility projection first**
   - Add `searchAttributeStringList` helper in `bin/lib/live-status.ts`.
   - Extend `ChangeSummary` type to include optional `worktreeBranches` and `worktreePaths` if not already typed.
   - Decode `AdvWorktreeBranches` and `AdvWorktreePaths` in `buildSummaryFromSearchAttributes`.
   - Add tests for multi-value KeywordList decoding.

2. **Remove routine fan-out**
   - Change `readDashboardAdvProject` so default routine path does not call `loadLiveStatus`/`loadOpsChanges`.
   - Keep injection seams for tests if useful, but default must be worker-free.
   - Add a regression test with a sentinel `loadOpsChanges` that fails if invoked during normal summary read.

3. **Adjust dashboard change model**
   - Carry `worktreePaths` into `DashboardAdvChange`.
   - Keep correlation branches from `summary.worktreeBranches`.
   - Treat `head_shas` as empty unless read-model/detail supplies it.

4. **Add detail API/page**
   - Add GET route parsing for `/change/{projectId}/{changeId}` and `/api/change/{projectId}/{changeId}`.
   - Reject non-GET with existing 405 pattern.
   - Use cached/read-model data for compact detail.
   - Add optional bounded single-change read helper for deeper data, not list+fan-out.
   - On timeout/failure, return degraded detail with copyable `adv_change_show` command.

5. **Update UI**
   - Make ADV cards anchors or clickable cards linking to local detail page.
   - Add detail rendering with compact default and expandable deeper context.
   - Render source-health degradation with source/project/repo/remediation context.
   - Preserve `safeUrl`, `escapeHtml`, and no mutation controls.

6. **Verification**
   - Targeted `bun test bin/lib/dashboard bin/lib/live-status.test.ts`.
   - Static/source check that routine dashboard default path no longer references `loadLiveStatus` or calls `getState` via dashboard read.
   - Live restart verification after archive: `/` and `/api/state` 200, detail route 200 for at least one visible card.

## LBP Analysis

The best long-term shape is a read-model dashboard:

- Summary refresh reads server-side Visibility metadata.
- Detail view may use richer reads only after explicit click.
- Optional details degrade locally and never reduce base dashboard availability.

This aligns with existing ADV architecture:
- `adv status` already has a worker-free Visibility requirement.
- Change workflows already upsert worktree branches/paths.
- Dashboard server already has GET-only/loopback and cache/coalescing seams.
- UI already has escaping and safe URL helpers.

It avoids overengineering:
- No new service.
- No new auth model.
- No broad persistence rewrite.
- No new search attributes until ops/head-SHA need is proven.

## Affected Components

- `bin/lib/live-status.ts`
  - Add array-aware search attribute reader.
  - Project worktree branches/paths into summaries.
  - Add one-change read helper only if detail route needs richer state.

- `bin/lib/types.ts`
  - Extend `ChangeSummary` with optional worktree branch/path fields if needed.

- `bin/lib/dashboard/adv.ts`
  - Remove default ops fan-out from routine project read.
  - Carry worktree paths.
  - Keep base ADV cards visible when optional fields are absent.

- `bin/lib/dashboard/server.ts`
  - Add detail route/API routing.
  - Keep GET-only rejection for state/detail paths.
  - Keep cache/coalescing for `/api/state`; detail reads are separate and bounded.

- `bin/lib/dashboard/ui.ts`
  - Link cards to detail page.
  - Render two-level detail view.
  - Improve source-health degraded cards.

- `bin/lib/dashboard/*test.ts`, `bin/lib/live-status.test.ts`
  - Add worker-free, branch/path, detail degradation, and safety coverage.

- `.adv/specs/advance-meta/spec.json` and docs mirror if needed
  - Add dashboard-specific worker-free/read-only routine refresh law or extend existing status CLI worker-free law.

## Design-Derived Criteria

1. Routine dashboard `/api/state` path must not call `loadLiveStatus`, `listLiveChangeStates`, or workflow `query(getState)` by default.
2. KeywordList decoding must preserve all string values and drop only blank/non-string values.
3. Detail route richer read must be scoped to one `(projectId, changeId)` and bounded by the existing dashboard/read timeout budget.
4. Detail route failure must not invalidate cached `/api/state` or project cards.
5. Detail route and state route must reject non-GET requests.
6. Source-health cards must include enough context to identify source and affected project/repo without exposing secret-bearing raw errors.
7. Card links must be local same-origin paths; external source links remain limited to `http:`/`https:` via `safeUrl`.

## Risks / Mitigations

- Risk: removing ops enrichment hides useful ops/head-SHA information.
  - Mitigation: detail route can fetch deeper state on demand; routine cards stay correct without it.

- Risk: direct detail route reload lacks cached source correlation.
  - Mitigation: detail API can rebuild compact state through same read-model path and filter one change; richer data is optional.

- Risk: `AdvWorktreeBranches`/`AdvWorktreePaths` absent for older workflows.
  - Mitigation: absence yields empty branches/paths; source correlation remains conservative and does not infer fuzzy matches.

- Risk: source-health details leak raw auth/token strings.
  - Mitigation: keep sanitized error codes/messages; tests assert token-like strings absent.

- Risk: spec surface is currently CLI-status-specific, not dashboard-specific.
  - Mitigation: add or extend spec law during implementation if design/prep confirms dashboard worker-free behavior should be durable beyond tests.

## Design Leverage Scout

- Candidates considered: 5.
- Auto-adopted: array-aware `searchAttributeStringList` next to existing helper; project existing `AdvWorktreeBranches`/`AdvWorktreePaths`; reuse existing dashboard injection/degraded seams; reuse GET-only/loopback/sanitization helpers.
- Surfaced/resolved: head-SHA/ops fate — demote to detail-only unless future evidence warrants new search attrs.
- Rejected/deferred: new decoder module and new Temporal search attributes for ops/head-SHA in this change.

## Validator Result

Validator: VALIDATED.

Findings:
- Correctness: design solves all objectives; load-bearing claims verified in code and Temporal docs.
- Simplicity: simplest viable path; no new attrs, decoder module, service, or auth model.
- Spec-law compliance: extends `rq-statusCliWorkerFree01` pattern; no contradiction.
- Alternatives: new ops/head-SHA search attrs and full read-model rewrite considered and rejected as premature.

Prep cautions:
- Reconcile AC3 wording (`omitted or degraded`) with design wording so acceptance matrix is unambiguous.
- Confirm detail route reuses existing loopback, GET-only, safe URL, and escaping guards.

# Executive Summary

Hardened the local ADV dashboard read path so routine `/api/state` refreshes use Temporal Visibility/read-model summaries instead of per-change workflow `getState` fan-out. ADV cards now expose safe local click-through detail pages with compact context, optional expandable deeper data, bounded one-change detail degradation, and copyable `adv_change_show` inspection commands.

Key outcomes:
- Added durable spec law `rq-dashboardWorkerFree01` plus docs mirror and contract test coverage.
- Decoded `AdvWorktreeBranches` and `AdvWorktreePaths` from Visibility into dashboard summaries.
- Removed routine ops/head-SHA enrichment fan-out from dashboard ADV reads; base cards remain visible when optional enrichment is unavailable.
- Added read-only detail API/page routes: `/api/change/{projectId}/{changeId}` and `/change/{projectId}/{changeId}`.
- Updated dashboard UI for local detail links, compact/deeper detail rendering, and source-health degradation cards.
- Preserved local-only/read-only/no-secret/no-raw-state-link constraints.

Verification:
- `bun test bin/lib/dashboard bin/lib/live-status.test.ts` — 68 pass.
- `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/cli-bridge-contract.test.ts` — 19 pass.
- `pnpm run schemas:check` — pass.
- Static source check — no `loadLiveStatus` or `getState` references under `bin/lib/dashboard`.
- Independent acceptance reviewer verdict — READY, no findings.
